Dark Phonics — SATPIN Pilot Readers
← Back to Dark Phonics
Weeks 1–6 (s, a, t, p, i, n) — original text and new photography, built only from letters already taught at each point. No text is copied from any published reader.
Week 1sWeek 2aWeek 3tWeek 4pWeek 5iWeek 6n
Week 1 s

Sound Only — s

Not enough letters exist yet to build a word, so this stays a sound-awareness week, just like your existing “s” week. Reuses your existing art wholesale — snake, sun, sock, star, soap, seal, saw, sandwich are already illustrated and hosted. No new art needed.

No new reader for this week — existing material carries over unchanged. Open the current live “s” week in Curriculum Studio →
Week 2 a

Sound Only — a

Same situation as Week 1 — reuses your existing “a” week art wholesale. No new art needed.

No new reader for this week — existing material carries over unchanged. Open the current live “a” week in Curriculum Studio →
Week 3 t

Where Is Segina?

Your existing reader already works perfectly here, unchanged. It's a readAloud week (decodability-exempt) and every asset already exists. No changes needed — this week ships as-is.

No new reader for this week — existing material carries over unchanged. Open “Where Is Segina?” in Curriculum Studio →
Week 4 p

Tap, Tap, Tap

Pool available: s, a, t, p. This is the tightest vocabulary point in the whole sequence — not enough nouns yet for a full narrative, so this is a short phrase reader. That's a legitimate, common shape at this exact point in a SATPIN sequence.

Page 1

Tap.

Page 2

Tap, tap, tap.

Page 3

A tap.

Page 4

Sap.

Page 5

Pat.

Page 6

Pat. Tap.

Page 7

Sat.

Page 8

Pat, tap, sat!

Back cover words: pat, tap, sap, sat, at, a
Week 5 i

Sit, Sip, Tip

Pool available: s, a, t, p, i. Still no narrator/character (no decodable name yet), but now there's enough for tiny sentences.

Page 1

Sit.

Page 2

Sit. Sit.

Page 3

A pit.

Page 4

Tip it.

Page 5

Sip it.

Page 6

It is a pit.

Page 7

Sit. Sip. Tip.

Back cover words: sit, pit, tip, sip, it, is, a
Week 6 n

I Sit. I Nap.

Pool available: s, a, t, p, i, n + the new glue word “I”. This is where your existing Ant character can come back — “ant” (a-n-t) is fully decodable now. Your old “n” book used “mat”, “cat”, and “am”, none of which are decodable yet in the new order (m and c come much later) — this rewrite avoids all three while keeping the same character, the same “I” glue-word celebration, and the same potato-gag ending.

Page 1

An ant.

Page 2

I sit.

Page 3

I sit in a pan.

Page 4

I sit in a tin.

Page 5

I nap.

Page 6

Tap, tap, tap!

🥔POTATO!
Page 7

Is it a…?!

Back cover words: I, an, ant, sit, in, pan, tin, nap, tap, is, it, a
